Oracle Events
OE 1: Search contacts for Jason Park using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Jason Park"). Discovers contact_id "realtor_4f36a2f81f34" (email: jason.park@mac.com, phone: (980) 226-2496, job: "Realtor -- Coldwell Banker", city: Charlotte) and a second record "npc_68c584a0d7a0" (email: jason.park@me.com, job: "Real Estate Agent -- Coldwell Banker").
OE 2: Search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Jason Park"). Discovers no dedicated CRM contact for Jason Park. A CRM contact for Erica Sutton (crm_crmcontact_63cb521f44b9) has engagement entries referencing a call with Jason Park, linking him to that borrower's file.
OE 3: Search Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Jason Park"). Note: realtor name may not be a supported search field; if no results, the agent should extract loan numbers from email correspondence (OE 7) and look up individual loans via mortgage_los_get_loan. Discovers 15 loans where Jason Park is listed as realtor spanning 2024-2026: LN-2024-00024 (Lauren Lee, $218K, closed, LO: Priya Desai), LN-2024-00046 ($243K, closed), LN-2024-00058 (Brian Mensah, $197K, closed), LN-2024-00072 (Hannah Singh, $573K, closed), LN-2024-00105 ($479K, closed), LN-2024-00108 (Miguel Scott, $337K, closed), LN-2025-00289 (Imani Reyes, $269K, closed), LN-2025-00309 ($170K, closed), LN-2025-00327 (Sandra Franklin, $422K, processing), LN-2025-00382 ($363K, closed), LN-2025-00392 ($325K, closed), LN-2025-00400 ($280K, closed), LN-2025-00432 ($396K, closed), LN-2025-00484 ($274K, closed), and LN-2026-00554 ($501K, clear_to_close, closing 2026-04-02, LO: Priya Desai). Volume is consistent 2024-2025 but drops sharply in 2026.
OE 4: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: ["crm_crmcontact_63cb521f44b9"]), also with contact_ids: ["crm_crmcontact_5611341df3bc"], or without filters. Multiple queries needed as engagements are tagged to different contacts. Discovers: crm_engagement_c39d9f0dd3e8 (CALL, 2026-03-31 13:50, "Realtor updated on delay" -- Jason Park unhappy about VVOE issue, buyer stressed, seller may get cold feet), crm_engagement_22125f512a3d (CALL, 2026-03-31 13:35, "Employment docs requested" -- Erica Sutton left Sutton Creative Group, freelancing), crm_engagement_b0ff10135268 (NOTE, 2026-03-31 14:22, "Guidelines under review"), crm_engagement_9cf037b88f0d (NOTE, contact: crm_crmcontact_5611341df3bc, 2026-03-31 13:25, "Employment change noted" -- VVOE failed, closing hold), crm_engagement_9c802692df6d (NOTE, no contact, 2026-03-31 13:20, "Status reverted" -- LOS suspended after failed VVOE).
OE 5: Search LOS for the VVOE failure loan using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_search_borrowers (query: "Erica Sutton"). Discovers LN-2026-00620 (loan_id: los_loan_f7130be9e770, borrower: Erica Sutton, status: underwriting, amount: $365K, purchase: $472,895, property: 2818 Albemarle Rd Charlotte NC 28215, rate lock expired 2026-03-28). Realtor on record is "Brian Allen" (not Jason Park), but CRM confirms Jason was called about this deal. Loan was CTC but reverted after VVOE revealed borrower changed employment.
OE 6: Retrieve activity log and conditions for LN-2026-00620 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_f7130be9e770") and/or mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_f7130be9e770"). Look for entries documenting the employment verification failure, CTC suspension, and new conditions for employment review.
OE 7: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Jason Park" or "jason.park@mac.com"). Discovers: (a) purchase agreement email to Elena Marchetti for 3222 Ballantyne Commons Pkwy at $269K (email_14742, March 2025), (b) title order emails CC'ing Jason on closings, (c) correspondence with Sofia Reyes re Hannah Singh and Jian Lombardi timelines, (d) correspondence with Darnell Price re William Washington (credit inquiry redirected), (e) correspondence with Andre Washington re Brian Mensah. Most recent email from Jason is May 2025 re Sandra Franklin closing (email_16278, LN-2025-00327). No 2026 email correspondence, confirming reduced engagement.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails (query: "VVOE" or "Erica Sutton" or "LN-2026-00620"). Discovers emails from Megan Holt at Commonwealth Wholesale (mholt@commonwealthwholesale.com) to Elena Marchetti, CC Priya Desai: email_email_38e3c5ada8b9 ("Suspended - LN-2026-00620 employment change") and email_email_4ce217145e2d ("Urgent: CTC revoked on LN-2026-00620"). Confirms borrower changed jobs right before closing.
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Jason" or "LN-2026-00620" or "VVOE"). Discovers: (a) Elena Marchetti (keystone_c6b42763fb1f) in #loan-processing (C002): "Got the formal UW email on LN-2026-00620. Suspended for employment change after VVOE. Looking ugly if she's 1099 now," (b) Priya Desai (keystone_930d770d3309) in #closings (C003, 2026-03-31 16:55): "Just updated Jason on LN-2026-00620. Kept it high level for now." Confirms the last interaction was someone calling Jason about the deal hitting a wall.
OE 10: Send email to Jason Park using send_email (sender: "jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["jason.park@mac.com"]). Content: warm, casual check-in valuing the partnership; not salesy; if a bad deal was found, acknowledge the snag without naming borrower details or throwing anyone under the bus. Tone matches Jordan Blake's personality.
OE 11: Log outreach in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or "EMAIL", body: note documenting Jordan Blake's check-in email to Jason Park re partner health and referral activity, with acknowledgment of recent closing delay). Creates the paper trail the prompt requests.
OE 12: Post in #sales-pipeline Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: brief message letting the team know Jordan is doing a health check on top partner accounts this week and asking them to flag anything relevant).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

3 The email body sent to jason.park@mac.com (visible in send_email parameters) expresses valuing the partnership or referral relationship with Jason Park, using warm and casual language rather than a sales pitch.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body is warm and casual throughout — it mentions genuinely enjoying working with Jason, not taking the relationship for granted, and invites coffee or lunch. There is no promotional or sales-oriented language.
✓Run #2The email body is warm and personal, calling Jason 'one of our most valued partners,' expressing that the relationship doesn't go unnoticed, and inviting lunch/coffee with no agenda. No sales or promotional language is present.
✓Run #3The email body includes 'You've been one of our most valued partners for a long time, and that's not something I take for granted' and 'it matters to me that your buyers are always getting the best experience.' Language is warm and relationship-focused, not salesy.
✓Run #4The email body says 'You've been one of our most valued partners over the years,' uses warm/casual language throughout, and invites coffee or lunch. No sales pitch language present.
✓Run #5The email body says 'You've been one of our most valued partners at Keystone for a while now, and that's not something I take for granted.' The overall tone is warm, relationship-focused, and not salesy.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states 'You've been one of our most valued partners at Keystone for a long time' and uses warm, casual, relationship-focused language throughout. No sales pitch is present.
4 The email body sent to jason.park@mac.com (visible in send_email parameters) acknowledges that one of Jason's recent files or referrals hit a snag, delay, or issue, without revealing borrower-specific details such as borrower names (e.g., Erica Sutton, Sandra Franklin), the VVOE failure, or the employment change.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body acknowledges 'one of your more recent referrals hit a snag on our side' without mentioning any borrower names (Sandra Franklin is not in the email), VVOE, or employment details. The acknowledgment is appropriately high-level.
✓Run #3The email body acknowledges 'I know we had a file recently that didn't end up working out the way anyone hoped' without mentioning Sandra Franklin, Erica Sutton, VVOE, or any borrower-specific details.
✓Run #4The email says 'I know the last file we worked on together hit a snag toward the end' — acknowledges the issue at a high level without naming borrowers (Sandra Franklin, Erica Sutton), mentioning VVOE, or disclosing employment details.
✓Run #5The email says 'I heard that one of your more recent files hit a snag on our end' without mentioning any borrower names, the VVOE failure, employment details, or Sandra Franklin specifically.
✓Run #6The email body says 'I know the last file we worked together on hit a snag toward the end' — acknowledging a problem at a high level. It does not mention borrower names (Sandra Franklin or Erica Sutton), VVOE, employment changes, or other borrower-specific details.
5 The email body sent to jason.park@mac.com (visible in send_email parameters) expresses wanting to make sure Keystone is still taking care of Jason's buyers the way he expects, or conveys a similar commitment to service quality.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email explicitly states 'Your buyers deserve a smooth experience, and so do you,' directly conveying the commitment to buyer care quality that the prompt requires.
✓Run #2The email states 'Your buyers deserve a smooth experience' and 'make sure we're still delivering the kind of service that made you comfortable working with us in the first place,' which directly conveys commitment to buyer care quality.
